    • Writing Recovery Methods for New Ordination Classes

Healthcare worker burnout data

hcw.Rd

Data from a 2022 longitudinal study by Guatello et al. on professional burnout and fulfillment in healthcare workers (HCW) during the Covid-19 pandemic.

Usage

hcw

Format

hcw

A data frame with 467 rows and 49 columns:

record_id

Subject identifier

hc_prov

Subject is a healthcare provider: 1 = TRUE, 0 = FALSE

occ

Occupation: 1 = physician, 2 = medical student, 3 = advanced practice worker (CRNA, NP, PA), 4 = nurse (LPN, RN, and trainees), 15 = dentist, 5 = physical therapist, 6 = occupational therapist, 7 = respiratory therapist, 11 = respiratory technician, 12 = radiology technician, 8 = psychologist, 9 = nursing assistant, 13 = sitter, 14 = transport, 10 = other occupation

pfi_01a

During the past two weeks, how true do you feel the following statement is about you at work: I feel happy at work (0 = not at all, 4 = extremely)

pfi_01b

During the past two weeks, how true do you feel the following statement is about you at work: I feel worthwhile at work (0 = not at all, 4 = extremely)

pfi_01c

During the past two weeks, how true do you feel the following statement is about you at work: My work is satisfying to me (0 = not at all, 4 = extremely)

pfi_01d

During the past two weeks, how true do you feel the following statement is about you at work: I feel in control when dealing with difficult problems at work (0 = not at all, 4 = extremely)

pfi_01e

During the past two weeks, how true do you feel the following statement is about you at work: My work is meaningful to me (0 = not at all, 4 = extremely)

pfi_01f

During the past two weeks, how true do you feel the following statement is about you at work: I'm contributing professionally (e.g. patient care, teaching, research, and leadership) in the ways I value most (0 = not at all, 4 = extremely)

pfi_02a

To what degree have you experienced the following: a sense of dread when I think about work I have to do (0 = not at all, 4 = extremely)

pfi_02b

To what degree have you experienced the following: physically exhausted at work (0 = not at all, 4 = extremely)

pfi_02c

To what degree have you experienced the following: lacking in enthusiasm at work (0 = not at all, 4 = extremely)

pfi_02d

To what degree have you experienced the following: emotionally exhausted at work (0 = not at all, 4 = extremely)

pfi_03a

During the past two weeks, my job has contributed to me feeling: less empathetic with my patients (0 = not at all, 4 = extremely)

pfi_03b

During the past two weeks, my job has contributed to me feeling: less empathetic with my colleagues (0 = not at all, 4 = extremely)

pfi_03c

During the past two weeks, my job has contributed to me feeling: less sensitive to others' feelings/emotions (0 = not at all, 4 = extremely)

pfi_03d

During the past two weeks, my job has contributed to me feeling: less interested in talking with my patients (0 = not at all, 4 = extremely)

pfi_03e

During the past two weeks, my job has contributed to me feeling: less connected with my patients (0 = not at all, 4 = extremely)

pfi_03f

During the past two weeks, my job has contributed to me feeling: less connected with my colleagues (0 = not at all, 4 = extremely)

phq9_01

Over the last two weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problem: little interest or pleasure in doing things (0 = not at all, 3 = nearly every day)

phq9_02

Over the last two weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problem: feeling down, depressed, or hopeless (0 = not at all, 3 = nearly every day)

phq9_03

Over the last two weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problem: trouble falling or staying asleep or sleeping too much (0 = not at all, 3 = nearly every day)

phq9_04

Over the last two weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problem: feeling tired or having little energy(0 = not at all, 3 = nearly every day)

phq9_05

Over the last two weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problem: poor appetite or overeating (0 = not at all, 3 = nearly every day)

phq9_06

Over the last two weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problem: feeling bad about yourself - or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down (0 = not at all, 3 = nearly every day)

phq9_07

Over the last two weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problem: trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television (0 = not at all, 3 = nearly every day)

phq9_08

Over the last two weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problem: moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed? Or the opposite - being so fidgety or restless that you have been moving around a lot more than usual (0 = not at all, 3 = nearly every day)

gad7_01

Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problems: feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge (0 = not at all, 3 = nearly every day)

gad7_02

Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problems: not being able to stop or control worrying (0 = not at all, 3 = nearly every day)

gad7_03

Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problems: worrying too much about different things (0 = not at all, 3 = nearly every day)

gad7_04

Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problems: having trouble relaxing (0 = not at all, 3 = nearly every day)

gad7_05

Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problems: being so restless that it's hard to sit still (0 = not at all, 3 = nearly every day)

gad7_06

Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problems: becoming easily annoyed or irritable (0 = not at all, 3 = nearly every day)

gad7_07

Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problems: feeling afraid as if something awful might happen (0 = not at all, 3 = nearly every day)

hcsystem_rate

I am rating my: 1 = direct supervisor, 2 = head of the health system, 3 = entire leadership team for the healthcare system that I work for

hcsystem_01

Easy to understand (0 = never, 4 = always)

hcsystem_02

Finds time to listen to group members

hcsystem_03

Refuses to explain their actions (0 = never, 4 = always)

hcsystem_04

Acts without consulting the group members (0 = never, 4 = always)

hcsystem_05

Willing to make changes (0 = never, 4 = always)

hcsystem_06

Makes group members feel at ease when talking with or to them (0 = never, 4 = always)

hcsystem_07

Puts suggestions made by the group into operation

hcsystem_08

Gets group input on important matters (0 = never, 4 = always)

hcsystem_09

Speaks in a manner not to be questioned (0 = never, 4 = always)

hcsystem_10

Makes sure that their part in the organization is understood by all group members (0 = never, 4 = always)

hcsystem_11

Asks that group members follow standard rules and regulations (0 = never, 4 = always)

hcsystem_12

Lets group members know what is expected of them (0 = never, 4 = always)

hcsystem_13

Sees to it that group members are working up to capacity (0 = never, 4 = always)

hcsystem_14

Sees to it that the work of all group members is coordinated (0 = never, 4 = always)

...

Source

https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35949164/

On this page

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
